questions.question: Which of the following quadrilaterals has rotational symmetry of order 2 but no lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A general parallelogram can be rotated 180° to look the same, but it cannot be folded along any line to match its halves perfectly.

questions.question: If a regular polygon has an angle of rotation of 72°, how many lines of symmetry does it have?
questions.explanation: Order of rotation = 360° / 72° = 5. For a regular polygon, lines of symmetry = order of rotation.

questions.question: Which English capital letter has exactly two lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 2?
questions.explanation: H has one vertical and one horizontal line of symmetry, and looks the same after a 180° rotation.

questions.question: The order of rotational symmetry of a kite is:
questions.explanation: A kite only looks like itself after a full 360° rotation (Order 1).

questions.question: A figure has rotational symmetry of order 4. Which of the following cannot be its angle of rotation?
questions.explanation: Angles of rotation must be multiples of (360/4) = 90°. 120° is not a multiple of 90°.

questions.question: Which triangle has both line symmetry and rotational symmetry of order greater than 1?
questions.explanation: Equilateral triangles have 3 lines of symmetry and rotational symmetry of order 3.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry does a regular polygon with an interior angle of 108° have?
questions.explanation: An interior angle of 108° belongs to a regular pentagon, which has 5 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: A rhombus is rotated about the intersection of its diagonals. At what minimum angle will it look the same as the original?
questions.explanation: A rhombus has rotational symmetry of order 2, so its minimum angle is 360/2 = 180°.

questions.question: Which of the following has only one line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: An isosceles trapezium has only one line of symmetry connecting the midpoints of the parallel sides.

questions.question: If a shape has rotational symmetry of order 3, what is its angle of rotation?
Which of the following letters has no line of symmetry but has rotational symmetry of order 2?
questions.explanation: S cannot be folded to match, but looks the same when turned upside down (180°).

questions.question: A regular polygon has 12 lines of symmetry. What is its angle of rotation?
questions.explanation: Lines of symmetry (12) = Order. Angle = 360/12 = 30°.

questions.question: For which of the following is the line of symmetry the perpendicular bisector of its sides?
questions.explanation: In a rectangle, lines of symmetry pass through the midpoints of opposite sides, bisecting them perpendicularly.

questions.question: The center of rotation for an equilateral triangle is the intersection of its:
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle, the orthocenter, centroid, and incenter are the same point, which acts as the center of rotation.

questions.question: Which of these figures has line symmetry but not rotational symmetry of order more than 1?
questions.explanation: Isosceles triangle has 1 line of symmetry but order 1 rotational symmetry.

questions.question: What is the order of rotational symmetry of a regular nonagon?
questions.explanation: A nonagon has 9 sides, so its rotational order is 9.

questions.question: How many lines of symmetry are there in a standard 'Star of David' (6-pointed star)?
questions.explanation: A 6-pointed star (hexagram) has 6 lines of symmetry.

questions.question: Which of the following is true for a circle?

options.C: It has an infinite order of rotational symmetry
options.D: It has only 2 lines of symmetry

questions.explanation: A circle looks the same at every possible infinitesimal rotation.

questions.question: If a figure is rotated by 45° and it looks the same, the order of rotational symmetry is:
Which of these shapes has the highest number of lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: Hexagon (6) > Pentagon (5) > Square (4) > Triangle (3).

questions.question: The angle of rotation of a figure is 18°. What is the order of rotational symmetry?
Which of the following quadrilaterals has lines of symmetry that do not pass through the vertices?
questions.explanation: Rectangle lines pass through midpoints of sides. Square has lines through midpoints AND vertices.

questions.question: A figure looks the same after 90° rotation. Does it necessarily look the same after 180° rotation?

options.C: Only if it is a square
options.D: Only if it has line symmetry

questions.explanation: If it matches at 90°, it will also match at every multiple of 90° (180°, 270°, 360°).

questions.question: Which of the following digits has both line and rotational symmetry?
questions.explanation: Assuming standard symmetrical fonts, 0, 8, and 1 possess both types of symmetry.

questions.question: What is the order of rotational symmetry of a windmill with 4 blades?
questions.explanation: A 4-bladed windmill matches its position every quarter turn (90°).
